content.query:
-- This is auto-generated code
SELECT
    TOP 100 *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'https://asynapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/electric-vehicle-data/bronze/vehicle.csv', --set the path of the file we want to read
        FORMAT = 'CSV', -- format specification, this also can be parquet or delta
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0' -- there are some differences between the versions, but those can be checked in the documentation 1.0 y 2.0
    ) AS [result];



-- This is auto-generated code
SELECT
    TOP 100 *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'abfss://electric-vehicle-data@asynapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/bronze/vehicle.csv', --this is the option abfss
        FORMAT = 'CSV', -- format specification, this also can be parquet or delta
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0', -- there are some differences between the versions, but those can be checked in the documentation 1.0 y 2.0
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE,
        FIELDTERMINATOR = ';',
        ROWTERMINATOR = '\n'

    ) AS [result];




    -- Examine datat types making use of a script procedure in synapse
EXECUTE sp_describe_first_result_set N'SELECT
    TOP 100 *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK ''abfss://electric-vehicle-data@asynapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/bronze/vehicle.csv'', --this is the option abfss
        FORMAT = ''CSV'', -- format specification, this also can be parquet or delta
        PARSER_VERSION = ''2.0'', -- there are some differences between the versions, but those can be checked in the documentation 1.0 y 2.0
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE

    ) AS [result];';

-- Fields len validation
SELECT 
    MAX(LEN(vehicleId)) AS vehicle_Id
    , MAX(LEN(vin)) AS vin
    , MAX(LEN(modelYear)) AS model_year
    , MAX(LEN(make)) AS make
    , MAX(LEN(model)) AS model
    , MAX(LEN(electricRange)) AS electric_range
    , MAX(LEN(baseMSRP)) AS baseMSRP
    , MAX(LEN(vehicheTypeId)) AS vehicle_type_id
    , MAX(LEN(cafvEligibilityId)) AS cafv_elegibility_id
    , MAX(LEN(vehicleLocation)) AS vehicle_location
    FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'abfss://electric-vehicle-data@asynapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/bronze/vehicle.csv', --this is the option abfss
        FORMAT = 'CSV', -- format specification, this also can be parquet or delta
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0', -- there are some differences between the versions, but those can be checked in the documentation 1.0 y 2.0
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
        ) AS [result];


-- Use WITH clause to provide explicit data type
SELECT TOP 100 *
FROM
OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'abfss://electric-vehicle-data@asynapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/bronze/vehicle.csv', --this is the option abfss
        FORMAT = 'CSV', -- format specification, this also can be parquet or delta
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0', -- there are some differences between the versions, but those can be checked in the documentation 1.0 y 2.0
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
        ) WITH (
            vehicleID           INT,
            vin                 VARCHAR(10),
            modelYEar           SMALLINT,
            make                VARCHAR(25), 
            model               VARCHAR(25),
            electricRange       SMALLINT,
            baseMSRP           INT,
            vehicheTypeId       SMALLINT,
            cafvEligibilityId   SMALLINT,
            vehicleLocation     VARCHAR(30)
            )
        AS [result];


--Validation of the recent applied formats

EXECUTE sp_describe_first_result_set N'
SELECT TOP 100 *
FROM
OPENROWSET(
        BULK ''abfss://electric-vehicle-data@asynapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/bronze/vehicle.csv'', --this is the option abfss
        FORMAT = ''CSV'', -- format specification, this also can be parquet or delta
        PARSER_VERSION = ''2.0'', -- there are some differences between the versions, but those can be checked in the documentation 1.0 y 2.0
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
        ) WITH (
            vehicleID           INT,
            vin                 VARCHAR(10),
            modelYEar           SMALLINT,
            make                VARCHAR(25), 
            model               VARCHAR(25),
            electricRange       SMALLINT,
            baseMSRP           INT,
            vehicheTypeId       SMALLINT,
            cafvEligibilityId   SMALLINT,
            vehicleLocation     VARCHAR(30)
            )
        AS [result]'


-- Create External Data Source: Folder at container level
CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE electric_vehicle_data
WITH(
    LOCATION = 'abfss://electric-vehicle-data@asynapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/'
-- access to the file with URL or ABfSS Path, folder and file name should be removed
-- this should be created in the database electric_vehicle_explorer
);

-- Example using DATA_SOURCE at container level
SELECT TOP 100 *
FROM
OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'bronze/vehicle.csv', --this is the option abfss
        DATA_SOURCE = 'electric_vehicle_data',
        FORMAT = 'CSV', 
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0', 
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
        ) WITH (
            vehicleID           INT,
            vin                 VARCHAR(10),
            modelYEar           SMALLINT,
            make                VARCHAR(25), 
            model               VARCHAR(25),
            electricRange       SMALLINT,
            baseMSRP           INT,
            vehicheTypeId       SMALLINT,
            cafvEligibilityId   SMALLINT,
            vehicleLocation     VARCHAR(30)
            )
        AS [result];

-- Create External Data Source: Folder at Folder level
CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE electric_vehicle_data_bronze
WITH(
    LOCATION = 'abfss://electric-vehicle-data@asynapseadls.dfs.core.windows.net/bronze/'
-- access to the file with URL or ABfSS Path, folder and file name should be removed
-- this should be created in the database electric_vehicle_explorer
);


-- Example using DATA_SOURCE at Folder level
SELECT TOP 100 *
FROM
OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'vehicle.csv', --this is the option abfss
        DATA_SOURCE = 'electric_vehicle_data_bronze',
        FORMAT = 'CSV', 
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0', 
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
        ) WITH (
            vehicleID           INT,
            vin                 VARCHAR(10),
            modelYEar           SMALLINT,
            make                VARCHAR(25), 
            model               VARCHAR(25),
            electricRange       SMALLINT,
            baseMSRP           INT,
            vehicheTypeId       SMALLINT,
            cafvEligibilityId   SMALLINT,
            vehicleLocation     VARCHAR(30)
            )
        AS [result];


-- How delete external data sources
DROP EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE electric_vehicle_data;

SELECT name, location FROM sys.external_data_sources
